channel.title: SwanSong for macOS Updates
channel.description: Developer ID signed and notarized SwanSong updates from GitHub Releases.

item.title: SwanSong 0.9.0
item.description:
Bring a trusted translation home to your library.

SwanSong 0.9 adds a local Translation Shelf for source-free, release-certified IPS packages.

  • Verify the exact path. SwanSong checks the package, IPS, required original revision, finished-game digest, cartridge checksum, and save contract.
  • Keep the original unchanged. Patching happens in memory, and only the verified translated result enters the private managed library.
  • Keep histories separate. The translated game receives its own identity, saves, states, artwork, favorite, and play history.
  • Repair without starting over. Rebuild a missing or changed managed copy while preserving its library history.
  • Developer tools stay developer-only. Agent playtest prompts and diagnostic confidence language remain hidden unless Developer Tools is enabled.

SwanSong 0.9.0 is the current stable release. It is offered on the default update channel.

SwanSong includes no games, translations, or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.8.1
item.description:
Your library stays focused on playing.

SwanSong 0.8.1 keeps internal compatibility confidence with Developer Tools and returns the everyday library to the essentials.

  • A quieter game shelf. Picture Appeared, Not Tested Yet, and Needs Attention stay hidden unless Developer Tools is enabled.
  • Testing tools stay together. The Game Confidence panel, verdict controls, notes, and related accessibility descriptions follow the same setting.
  • Everyday controls remain ready. Play, repair, game details, and save controls stay available.
  • Your evidence is preserved. Hiding developer surfaces does not erase saved compatibility work.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.8.0
item.description:
Prove the whole game, one named surface at a time.

SwanSong 0.8 turns Translation Lab captures into complete, resumable surface suites with exact native review and one immutable certification handoff.

  • Run the whole matrix. Case-specific viewer ROMs, input plans, and named checkpoints execute from clean boot on the selected qualified ABI.
  • Catch obvious misses early. Wrong screens, zero delta, stale routes, and protected-region changes fail before human review.
  • Review what the handheld shows. Original, Patched, blink, and difference views stay at exact native 1× with separate meaning, microcopy, and fit verdicts.
  • Keep quality human. Narrative Pulse, live-reader moments, and Story Proof expose evidence without manufacturing an approval score.
  • Enjoy a clearer Homebrew shelf. Bundled title screens and a compact native inspector make signed catalog entries easier to browse.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.7.2
item.description:
Translation evidence keeps its real source.

SwanSong 0.7.2 makes authenticated capture plans follow their qualified engine profile and keeps General DMA cartridge lineage exact through nested callbacks.

  • Use the authorized engine profile. Capture plans support qualified ABI 9 and ABI 10 lanes while rejecting profile or engine drift.
  • Keep DMA provenance honest. General DMA reads retain their real initiator, source operand, and cartridge origin.
  • Catch regressions without game data. A clean-room public fixture covers exact DMA lineage in the live-engine release lane.
  • Keep private evidence private. Game addresses, source bytes, mapper state, and project details remain local.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.7.1
item.description:
Translation Lab can follow the pixels.

SwanSong 0.7.1 gives source probing a signed, capture-authorized path from a visible rectangle back to its real cartridge lineage—while private game details stay inside your project.

  • Probe the frame you captured. The project, ROM, plan, frame, rectangle, helper, runner, and engine must all agree.
  • Change with confidence. Select map, raster, palette, or sprite ownership and still discover other display elements sharing the same source.
  • Keep private evidence private. Automation receives safe counts, hashes, geometry, and completeness—not addresses, cartridge ranges, mapper state, or source bytes.
  • Trust the finished proof. SwanSong writes the closure last, reads it back, and validates the complete evidence tree.
  • Skip the password box. Runtime and local-tool release gates keep ordinary SwanSong use away from the login Keychain.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.7.0
item.description:
A safer SwanSong—without more fuss.

SwanSong 0.7 puts the game engine in its own locked-down service, hardens local automation, adds a launch-rescuing Safe Mode, and creates privacy-safe Support Bundles.

  • Play behind a stronger boundary. The isolated engine accepts only typed, size-limited data and has no network access.
  • Trust local automation. Fresh, one-use requests must come from SwanSong's signed helper and the current Mac user.
  • Get unstuck. Safe Mode skips restored work and background helpers without touching your library or saves.
  • Ask for help safely. Support Bundles leave out games, saves, screenshots, projects, private paths, and accounts.
  • Trace translations farther. Deeper source provenance stays private while MCP receives only counts, hashes, geometry, and completeness.

Every release now includes exact source, checksums, an SPDX software bill of materials, and verifiable artifact attestations.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.6.1
item.description:
SwanSong is ready before you are.

SwanSong 0.6.1 opens faster, keeps an old catalog trust record from interrupting launch, and helps Studio find the modern Python already installed on your Mac.

  • See your library sooner. The main window appears first while controllers, local tools, and the workshop finish waking up behind it.
  • Skip the surprise password box. Homebrew Catalog trust checks now fail quietly instead of asking for the Mac login password.
  • Open Studio from Finder. SwanSong finds Python 3.11+ in standard Homebrew, python.org, MacPorts, and system locations.
  • Keep everything from 0.6. Studio, Story Forge, Translation Lab, Time Ribbon, save states, and the native player are all here.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Development tools remain separate prerequisites. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.6.0
item.description:
The whole SwanSong workshop can move with you.

SwanSong 0.6.0 brings the complete SDK 0.5 Studio, a much richer Story Forge, and frame-authenticated Translation Lab automation to the public Mac app.

  • Build more in Studio. Create Utility Apps, run traced builds and scenarios, inspect outcomes and budgets, migrate projects, and finish an evidence-backed release.
  • Write in a real Story Room. Map the story, edit chapters, preserve revisions, work with unprimed readers and research, audition art and music, and prepare a source-mapped WonderSwan adaptation.
  • Trust the exact captured frame. Capture-bound source probes validate the private native frame before provenance begins and leave nothing partial behind on a mismatch.
  • Enjoy calmer local tools. Trusted automation no longer needs to touch the login keychain, and release builds gain a sealed, reproducible engine gate.

SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Studio and Story Forge use separately installed development tools or selected local projects. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.5.0
item.description:
The workshop just got bigger.

SwanSong 0.5.0 adds a native writing desk and a guarded bridge to real WonderSwan cartridges.

  • Write from idea to edition. Story Forge keeps outline, draft, revision, art, continuity, rights, EPUB, and PDF work together.
  • Continue into a game. Hand a revision-ready novel to SwanSong Studio for a WonderSwan adaptation.
  • Back up real cartridges. Cartridge Lab makes verified ROM and save backups through a WonderSwan Color or SwanCrystal.
  • Restore saves carefully. Exact sizing, two-screen confirmation, physical A+B approval, and complete readback guard every write.
  • Enjoy a clearer SwanSong. The README, setup guides, FAQ, Support, and Wiki now lead with what you can do.

Story Forge and Cartridge Lab require separately obtained local projects or authorized hardware. SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.4.3
item.description:
Every press reaches the game.

SwanSong 0.4.3 makes exact-frame controls reliable, brings SwanSong SDK 0.4.0 into Studio, and makes the app's main screens easier to understand.

  • Trust short inputs. Presses and releases reach the live WonderSwan keypad on their scheduled frame.
  • Build on SDK 0.4.0. Studio content-verifies the released runtime, schema, recipes, docs, and Python package.
  • Keep games responsive. Normal frame presentation no longer performs expensive whole-map and whole-sprite profiler scans.
  • Find the next action. Library, Homebrew, Translation Lab, Studio, Settings, Updates, and Support lead with clearer language.

Python and Wonderful remain local prerequisites. SwanSong includes no games or original system firmware. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.4.2
item.description:
Make the next game smaller, clearer, better.

SwanSong 0.4.2 brings the released SwanSong SDK 0.3.1 toolset into Studio, with native controls for visual authoring, replay inspection, and deterministic failure reduction.

  • Shape the game. Create, validate, report, and export tilemap, sprite, palette, collision, scene-flow, and audio documents.
  • Read the replay. Turn exact-frame plans, checkpoints, evidence, and traces into one read-only timeline.
  • Shrink the failure. Delta-reduce a failing plan through bounded fresh SwanSong runs and verify the failure still holds.
  • Trust the bundle. Studio content-verifies the exact tagged SDK runtime, schema, recipes, docs, and Python package.
  • Finish Doctor cleanly. Its bounded local SwanSong probe no longer waits for a valid persistent server to exit.

Python and Wonderful remain local prerequisites, and authoring or replay reports do not replace inspected gameplay evidence. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.4.1
item.description:
Follow the source.

SwanSong 0.4.1 gives Translation Lab an ABI 9 path from a visible sprite to its private OAM ownership and cartridge lineage, plus a guarded handoff to static-analysis tools.

  • Trace sprites directly. Select sprite attributes independently from map, raster, and palette components.
  • Keep provenance private. OAM addresses, CPU writers, cartridge ranges, mapper context, and conservative origins stay inside the project.
  • Start analysis from a fact. Export a deterministic private Ghidra or pypcode seed from a current, complete source probe.
  • Handle real evidence safely. Source artifacts may grow to 64 MiB and 256 normalized ranges while uncertain lineage still fails closed.
  • Preserve prior work. The Evidence browser continues to read v1-v3 source artifacts after the move to ABI 9 schema v4.

Restart SwanSong and the MCP client after updating. Static-analysis seeds do not authorize patches, and private project evidence never leaves through SwanSong's tool responses. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.4.0
item.description:
Build where you play.

SwanSong 0.4 adds Studio for the complete SwanSong SDK workflow and gives Translation Lab a deeper, private path from visible pixels to cartridge sources.

  • Make and iterate. Create a recipe, build assets, test, play, profile, inspect evidence, and package from one native workspace.
  • Turn play into proof. Record deterministic scenarios, compare visual and audio evidence, and require inspected results before release.
  • Trace upstream privately. Select map, raster, or palette provenance while exact cartridge and executed-read details stay inside the project.
  • Use the whole toolset. The installed plugin exposes persisted capture, both probes, record/verify, and the complete observed-play lifecycle.
  • Find the swan. SwanSong now has its own compact menu-bar status item.

Studio is a developer preview that uses SwanSong SDK 0.2 or newer and a separately installed toolchain. Your projects, ROMs, diagnostics, and evidence stay on your Mac. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.3.1
item.description:
See exactly what changed.

SwanSong 0.3.1 turns Translation Lab into a workspace that remembers the whole investigation.

  • Keep every comparison. Original and Patched frames, replay plans, bindings, and pixel differences stay together inside your project.
  • Trace the pixels. Select a rectangle to learn which layer, map cells, tiles, palette, and final writer produced it.
  • Play at your pace. Observed sessions advance one visible step at a time while preserving clean from-boot proof.
  • Read without squinting. Support, Privacy, and Acknowledgements now use real headings, lists, links, and spacing.

Private by design. Detailed evidence stays in the project you choose. Read the full release notes.

item.title: SwanSong 0.3.0
item.description: This signed update is distributed through the official SwanSong GitHub release. Open the release page for complete notes and checksums.

item.title: SwanSong 0.2.0
item.description: This signed update is distributed through the official SwanSong GitHub release. Open the release page for complete notes and checksums.
